response.justification: While the candidate is a strong engineer with excellent software practices and leadership experience, they do not meet the critical minimum qualifications for this role. The position requires 8 years of C++ programming experience; the resume shows only ~2 years (National Instruments 2014-2015). Additionally, the candidate's background is predominantly in full-stack web, mobile, and product development, not large-scale cloud compute infrastructure. The combination of insufficient C++ depth and lack of proven experience building/leading compute platforms makes this a poor fit despite strong general engineering credentials.
strengths: Demonstrates strong software engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, code quality focus) and has successfully mentored engineers through workshops and training courses
Proven ability to own ambiguous technical projects end-to-end, from GISCollective to multiple roles requiring full-stack ownership
Currently in a Staff-level role with diverse technical breadth across multiple languages and platforms, showing adaptability and technical depth
concerns: Only ~2 years of C++ experience versus the required 8 years—a critical gap for a role where C++ is the primary language

Background is heavily weighted toward web/full-stack development (Node.js, TypeScript, React) with minimal demonstrated experience building large-scale cloud compute infrastructure or platform systems
No clear evidence of technical leadership specifically on cloud/infrastructure/DevOps teams or experience managing distributed compute platforms
